gov.tools.title: Tool Governance
gov.tools.networkPolicyAllowedDomainsHin: Wildcards supported, e.g. *.openai.com. One domain per line.
gov.tools.activeToolRefHint: Tool ref version to activate, e.g. builtin:http_request@v2
gov.tools.networkPolicyToolRefHint: Enter tool ref to load/edit its network policy
gov.tools.hint.toolRef: Unique tool reference identifier in the format namespace:name, e.g. builtin:http_request
gov.tools.hint.scope: Tool scope: space (space-level) applies to current space only, tenant (tenant-level) applies globally
gov.tools.hint.toolName: Internal name identifier of the tool, used to reference the tool definition within the system
gov.tools.hint.activeToolRef: Currently active tool version reference, e.g. builtin:http_request@v2, where @version is the version number
gov.tools.hint.allowedDomains: Whitelist of external domains the tool is allowed to access, one per line. Supports wildcards like *.openai.com. Unlisted domains will be blocked.
gov.tools.col.name: Name
gov.tools.graceHint: Existing runs can continue during the grace period, and new runs are rejected immediately
gov.models.title: Model Onboarding
gov.models.hint.provider: Select an AI model provider. The system will auto-fill the default baseUrl and path.
gov.models.hint.baseUrl: The API endpoint URL of the model service, usually starts with https://, e.g. https://api.deepseek.com
gov.models.hint.chatPath: Path for the chat completions endpoint. OpenAI-compatible services typically use /chat/completions
gov.models.hint.apiKey: API key for the model service, used for authentication. Keys are securely encrypted and never displayed in plain text.
gov.models.hint.modelName: The specific model identifier to use, e.g. deepseek-v3, gpt-4o, qwen-turbo
gov.models.connector.name: Connector name
gov.channels.title: Conversation Channels
gov.channels.identityHint: Note: Conversation Channels manage 'IM message bridging' — which platforms the agent receives messages from. This is independent of 'Identity Sources' — a user can login via WeChat but converse through DingTalk.
gov.channels.provider.hint: Select the IM platform to connect
gov.channels.workspaceId.hint: e.g. Feishu App ID, DingTalk corpId, etc.
gov.channels.spaceId.hint: The agent space identifier, found in Space Management
gov.channels.secretEnvKey.hint: Environment variable storing the platform secret, e.g. FEISHU_APP_SECRET
gov.channels.secretId.hint: If using the secret vault, enter the secret ID; otherwise leave blank
gov.channels.chatBinding.desc: Bind an IM group chat to an agent space so the agent responds to group messages
gov.channels.channelChatId.hint: The group ID from the IM platform
gov.channels.defaultSubjectId.hint: Messages from unbound users will use this identity
gov.channels.accountBinding.desc: Bind IM users to platform subjects for cross-channel identity and memory sharing
gov.channels.channelUserId.hint: The user's unique ID on the IM platform
gov.channels.binding.title: QR Code Authorization Binding
gov.channels.binding.desc: Generate an authorization link. IM users scan the QR code to auto-bind their channel identity, no manual channelUserId entry needed.
gov.channels.binding.label: Label (optional)
gov.channels.binding.prerequisite: Please select an IM platform, enter a workspace ID, and choose a space
gov.channels.binding.noConfigWarning: No channel config found for the selected platform and workspace. Please save a config in the Channel Config tab first, or QR binding will fail.
gov.channels.binding.qrHint: Scan the QR code below with your IM app to authorize binding
gov.channels.binding.col.label: Label
gov.channels.step.provider.desc: Choose a conversation channel (independent from login identity — one user can talk to the agent through multiple channels)
gov.channels.step.credentials.desc: Enter channel credentials to establish a message bridge. One MindPal instance can connect multiple channels simultaneously.
gov.channels.step.mapping.desc: Configure chat binding or user identity mapping (can be done later)
gov.channels.configEmpty.hint: Select an IM platform above and fill in credentials to create your first channel

gov.workflowDeadletters.title: Failed Tasks
gov.workflowDeadletters.subtitle: Workflow tasks that failed and exceeded retry limit
gov.workflowDeadletters.diagnose.title: Diagnosis
gov.workflowDeadletters.diagnose.timeout:
• Possible: Tool execution timeout
• Suggestion: Check network, increase timeout, or contact tool provider
gov.workflowDeadletters.diagnose.auth:
• Possible: Invalid credentials or insufficient permissions
• Suggestion: Check if API key expired, verify role permissions
gov.workflowDeadletters.diagnose.rateLim:
• Possible: Rate limit exceeded
• Suggestion: Wait before retrying, or request quota increase
gov.workflowDeadletters.diagnose.validat:
• Possible: Invalid input parameters
• Suggestion: Verify tool parameter definitions and input format
gov.workflowDeadletters.diagnose.notFoun:
• Possible: Tool or resource deleted
• Suggestion: Verify tool reference and resource existence
gov.workflowDeadletters.diagnose.unknown:
• Unknown error type
• Suggestion: Review error digest, contact system administrator

gov.routing.title: Routing Policy
gov.routing.subtitle: Assign dedicated models for different purposes. E.g. use a lightweight model for intent classification, a high-quality model for conversation.
gov.routing.purposeHelp: Select a system preset or enter a custom purpose identifier
gov.routing.primaryModelRefHelp: All LLM calls for this purpose will be routed to this model
gov.routing.noBindings: No bound models found. Please bind a model in [Settings > Model Access] first.
gov.routing.purpose.intent.classify.desc: Fast intent screening for every user message. Use a lightweight model to reduce latency and cost.
gov.routing.purpose.intent.analyze.desc: LLM call for the intent analyzer, used for more precise intent recognition.
gov.routing.purpose.orchestrator.turn.de: Core conversation model, used for user dialogue and Q&A.
gov.routing.purpose.agent.loop.think.des: Core decision reasoning when agents execute tasks.
gov.routing.purpose.agent.loop.think.fas: Fast progression for simple steps, can use lightweight model.
gov.routing.purpose.agent.loop.decompose: Break down complex goals into executable steps.

admin.sso.title: Identity Sources
admin.sso.desc: Manage trusted login identity sources for this instance (independent from conversation channels, controls 'who can log in')
admin.sso.noProviders: No identity sources configured. Personal instances can add WeChat/Alipay login; enterprise instances can use DingTalk/Feishu/WeCom OIDC.
admin.sso.form.hint.wechat: WeChat Open Platform: Create a website application at open.weixin.qq.com to get AppID and AppSecret. Platform URL is auto-filled.
admin.sso.form.hint.alipay: Alipay Login: Create an application at open.alipay.com to get AppID. Platform URL is auto-filled.
admin.sso.form.autoProvisionDesc: Automatically create user accounts on first login
admin.sso.instructions.title: Identity Sources vs Channels
admin.sso.instructions.oidc: Identity sources control 'who can log in': create an app in your IdP (DingTalk, Feishu, WeCom, WeChat, Azure AD, etc.), obtain Client ID and Secret, configure the callback URL. Note: this is for login authentication, independent from IM message channels configured in 'Conversation Channels'.
admin.sso.instructions.saml: SAML: Upload SAML metadata from your IdP or manually configure SSO URL and certificate.
admin.scim.title: User Directory Sync
admin.scim.desc: Auto-sync users from external directories to this instance (works with Identity Sources for complete identity management)
admin.scim.noConfigs: No SCIM configurations. Click the button above to add one.
admin.scim.tokenWarning: Please save this token immediately. It will not be shown again.
admin.scim.instructions.title: Directory Sync Guide
admin.scim.instructions.auth: Configure SCIM endpoint and Bearer Token in your IdP. Identity Sources handle 'how to log in', Directory Sync handles 'where users come from' — together they complete the identity management loop.
admin.scim.instructions.idp: Supports DingTalk, Feishu, WeCom, Azure AD, Okta, OneLogin and other major identity providers

alert.error.INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR: Internal server error, please retry later or contact an administrator

alert.error.CONNECTION_ERROR: Unable to connect to server, please check your network
alert.error.MODEL_NOT_AVAILABLE: Model service unavailable, please check API key and endpoint

alert.recovery.RATE_LIMITED: Suggestion: Wait a few seconds and try again
alert.recovery.INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR: Suggestion: If the error persists, contact technical support
alert.recovery.TIMEOUT: Suggestion: Check your network or try refreshing the page

alert.recovery.CONNECTION_ERROR: Suggestion: Check your network connection and refresh
